core_model.structure_summary: An equilibrium probabilistic model over two interleaved chains of binary units on a one-dimensional lattice, with an energy consisting of nearest-neighbor pairwise products within one chain, cross-chain pairwise products coupling each unit of the second chain to two adjacent units of the first, and linear external-field terms with different coefficients per chain, whose thermal averages exhibit a sharp switch between two ordered configurations at a temperature set by a transcendental balance condition.
core_model.canonical_form: E = -J\sum_i x_i x_{i+1} - J_1 \sum_i (x_i + x_{i+1}) y_i - h_a \sum_i x_i - h_b \sum_i y_i, \quad x_i, y_i \in \{\pm 1\}, \quad \langle x_i \rangle = -\partial f / \partial h_a

variables.meaning: binary units on the first sublattice with nearest-neighbor self-coupling
binary units on the second sublattice, each coupled to two adjacent first-sublattice units
external linear field with distinct weights on the two sublattices
inverse temperature controlling the equilibrium distribution
free energy whose field-derivatives give the average unit values

notes: Core model is a decorated one-dimensional binary-unit chain in thermal equilibrium at a critical external field, solved exactly (transfer-matrix-style); the key result is a degenerate configuration (one sublattice fully ordered, the other fully disordered, i.e. maximal entropy) and its hidden twin driving an abrupt switch of thermal averages at a finite crossover temperature given by a transcendental equation. The switch is a sharp crossover, tagged phase_transition/threshold_nonlinearity, even though a true finite-temperature singularity is forbidden in one dimension.
